data: Coarse-grained, homogeneous, white marble, likely Aphrodisian, Parian-2, Proconnesian, Thasian-1, Thasian-2, Parian-3. Digital microscopy: heteroblastic to homeoblastic fabric (identification based on pXRF, digital microscopy)
Christian; tentative identification of the bishop Syrakosios mentioned, with bishop in letter of Pope Gelasios, 492—6 BCE (?)

I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo Museo Archeologico Regionale Paolo Orsi

An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia
S.L. Agnello, Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia (Rome, 1953)

Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Istituto nazionale di archeologia e storia dell’arte (Italy), e Reale Accademia d’Italia, «Notizie degli scavi di antichità», Notizie degli Scavi di Antichità, 1876, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1646037
La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Giacomo Manganaro, «La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano», Aufstieg und Niedergang der romischen Welt 2.11.1 (1988): 3–89
Robert Crellin integrated lemmatizations into EpiDoc using PyEpiDoc and associated scripts
Agnello, 1953, Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia
Manganaro, 1988, La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
